problem: Where should session tokens live on the client?
decision: Store the JWT in an HttpOnly, SameSite=Lax cookie; never in localStorage.
alternatives: localStorage with manual Authorization header wiring
in-memory only, re-auth on page reload
constraints: All first-party clients are on the same registrable domain
CSRF is mitigated via SameSite=Lax + origin check
failures: Token exfiltration via third-party script on the page
Silent loss of session on reload
expiry_conditions: We ship a subdomain-isolated client that needs cross-site auth
We move to opaque session ids stored server-side

future_reminders.who_might_repeat: engineer adding a new SSO provider or mobile client
future_reminders.revisit_when: a client needs tokens accessible to JavaScript (e.g. cross-origin fetch without proxy)
